completed_tasks.name: Phase 02 fully verified — all 14 conditions passed
SGM consumer classification fix — demand/graph modes for pure-gpu windows

remaining_tasks.name: In-game: verify SGM demand mode gives proportional shares between Miner and Scheduler (not equal)
In-game: verify SGM graph mode shows real demand Hz after fix
Update 02-03-SUMMARY.md with SGM consumer fix details if verified

blockers.description: SGM consumer fix needs in-game verification — Miner and Scheduler must show different allocation in Demand mode

human_actions_pending.action: Relaunch Upload Labs. Connect SGM to Miner + Scheduler. Switch to Demand mode. Confirm: do Miner and Scheduler receive DIFFERENT amounts of gpu_speed (proportional to their required values), not equal?
human_actions_pending.context: Fix applied in 82add79: SGM stm_window_data.gd now classifies windows as STM_CONSUMER when !provided.is_empty() (was: required dependent non-STM inputs). get_demand() now falls back to provided container required value. Role re-evaluated in set_containers() so it updates when STM connections arrive.

decisions.decision: SGM consumer classification changed to !provided.is_empty() — any window receiving from SGM is a consumer
decisions.rationale: Pure gpu_speed windows (Miner, Scheduler) have no other resource inputs, so dependent=[] always. Old classification (requires dependent non-STM inputs) meant they were always STORAGE -> equal distribution in all modes. New classification uses provided container required value as demand proxy.

decisions.decision: STM left unchanged — STM working correctly with Virus Scanner and Quarantine (multi-resource windows)
decisions.rationale: User confirmed STM works. STM downstream windows have other resource inputs -> dependent populated -> correct consumer classification already.

decisions.decision: Role re-evaluated in set_containers() not _init() for SGM
decisions.rationale: provided is empty at _init() time (no sources passed). If role set at init with !provided.is_empty(), all windows start as STORAGE. Moving role assignment to set_containers() means it re-evaluates when actual connection IDs arrive.

next_action: Ask user: did SGM Demand mode give Miner and Scheduler DIFFERENT gpu_speed amounts after relaunch? If yes, update 02-03-SUMMARY.md and mark complete.
context_notes: Phase 02 is functionally complete. STM fully verified. SGM had a secondary bug: consumer classification required dependent (non-STM) inputs, but GPU windows only have gpu_speed input -> always STORAGE -> demand/graph = ratio always. Fix applied and installed (18605 bytes ZIP). Verification pending.
